agent_details.description: Syncs, triages, and closes GitHub issues for canvas_toolbox as a local markdown-based workflow.

agent_type.description: Step-by-step workflow: sync → triage → fix → close. API calls to GitHub REST API for read/write on issues.

steps.name: Check Sprint
steps.description: Read agile_sprint.md — find the active sprint and the next [ ] issue in order. Do not pick work outside sprint order without a reason.

steps.name: Sync
steps.description: Pull all open issues + comments from GitHub into .github_issues/open/ as markdown files
steps.input: GH_TOKEN, repo (auto-detected or GITHUB_REPO env var)

steps.name: Read Issue
steps.description: Open the issue file for the sprint's next item. Read description and all comments before writing any code.

steps.name: Fix
steps.description: Edit code. Do not commit yet — QC must pass first.

steps.name: QC
steps.description: Run sprint QC from sprint_qc.md against CANVAS_SANDBOX_ID. Level 0 (smoke), Level 1 (sandbox pull), Level 2 (sprint-specific tests), Level 3 (quality check). All must pass before committing.
steps.input: CANVAS_SANDBOX_ID, sprint_qc.md tests for current sprint

steps.name: Commit
steps.description: Only after QC passes — commit with message referencing the issue ('fixes #N')

steps.name: Close
steps.description: Run gh_close.py --issue N --comment 'Fixed in commit <hash>.' to post comment, close on GitHub, move local file to closed/
steps.output: Closed issue on GitHub + file in .github_issues/closed/

steps.name: Update Sprint
steps.description: Mark the issue [x] in agile_sprint.md with commit hash. If all sprint issues are [x], run full QC sign-off, then tag per semantic_versioning.md and move sprint to Completed Sprints.
steps.output: Updated sprint tracking + optional version tag

steps.name: Re-sync
steps.description: Run gh_sync.py to confirm all sprint issues are in closed/.

workflow_based.exit_point: All open/ files resolved; each closed on GitHub with a commit reference

api_agent.rate_limiting: 5,000 requests/hour for authenticated users. Check X-RateLimit-Remaining header.

inputs.name: GITHUB_REPO
inputs.notes: Auto-detected from git remote origin if not set

inputs.name: close_comment
inputs.example: Fixed in commit abc123. Canvas sync now handles empty modules correctly.

outputs.name: closed_issue_files
outputs.conditions: Written on gh_close.py run or when issue is closed externally

io_contract.side_effects: gh_close.py posts a comment and changes issue state on GitHub (irreversible via script — reopen manually if needed)

label_taxonomy.description: Priority order and handling rules for GitHub issue labels

labels.label: bug
labels.handling: Fix before any enhancement work. Every open bug blocks a potential user.
labels.close_template: Fixed in commit {hash}. {one-line description of what was broken and what changed}.

labels.label: enhancement
labels.handling: Batch by tool affected (canvas_sync, blueprint_sync, course_quality_check, etc.). Work one tool's enhancements together.
labels.close_template: Implemented in commit {hash}. {brief description of what was added and how it works}.

labels.label: documentation
labels.handling: Quick wins — update README, CLAUDE.md.example, or agent guides. Usually resolvable in a single pass.
labels.close_template: Updated in commit {hash}. {what was clarified or added}.

labels.label: question
labels.handling: Answer in a comment, then close. If the answer reveals a missing doc, open a documentation issue.
labels.close_template: Answered above. Feel free to reopen if you need more detail.

labels.label: duplicate
labels.handling: Close immediately with a reference to the canonical issue.

labels.label: wontfix
labels.handling: Close with a clear explanation of why the request is out of scope.
labels.close_template: Closing as out of scope: {reason}. {alternative if any}.

labels.label: good first issue
labels.handling: Leave open for contributors. Add a comment with pointers to relevant files if one is not already there.

api_endpoints.name: list_open_issues
api_endpoints.notes: Returns both issues and PRs. Filter by absence of 'pull_request' key.

api_endpoints.name: list_comments
api_endpoints.notes: Paginated. Fetch all pages if issue.comments > 100.

api_endpoints.name: check_rate_limit
api_endpoints.notes: Returns remaining requests and reset timestamp. Use to check before large syncs.

issue_file_format.description: YAML frontmatter + markdown body written by gh_sync.py
issue_file_format.slug_rules: lowercase, spaces→hyphens, non-alphanumeric removed, max 60 chars

environment_variables.name: GITHUB_REPO
environment_variables.description: owner/repo — auto-detected from git remote if not set

environment_variables.name: CANVAS_SANDBOX_ID
environment_variables.description: Canvas course ID of the sandbox course used for sprint QC testing — safe to read/write during validation runs

pre_run_checklist: Token has repo or public_repo scope
.github_issues/ folder exists or will be created by gh_sync.py
post_run_checklist: gh_sync: .github_issues/open/ contains files matching open issues on GitHub
gh_sync: each file has valid YAML frontmatter with issue number, title, url
gh_close: issue shows as closed on github.com
gh_close: file moved from open/ to closed/
gh_close: comment with commit reference visible on GitHub issue
success_criteria: gh_sync.py exits 0 and prints count of issues synced
gh_close.py exits 0 and confirms comment posted + issue closed
No issue files remain in open/ after all bugs are resolved

test_cases.name: Sync with no open issues
test_cases.expected_output: Sync complete: 0 open, 0 moved to closed/

test_cases.name: Close with comment
test_cases.expected_output: Comment posted to #42 + Issue #42 closed on GitHub + file moved to closed/

known_failures.cause: More than 5,000 API requests in an hour (unlikely unless repo has hundreds of issues with heavy comment threads)
known_failures.mitigation: Check X-RateLimit-Remaining header. Wait until X-RateLimit-Reset timestamp before retrying.

known_failures.cause: GH_TOKEN does not have write access to issues
known_failures.mitigation: Regenerate token with repo or public_repo scope. gh_close.py will return 403.

known_failures.cause: git remote origin is not set or is not a GitHub URL

when_to_use: At the start of each dev session — check agile_sprint.md first, then sync issues
After pushing a fix — close the issue and update agile_sprint.md
When a sprint completes — mark it done, review lessons learned, confirm next sprint order
When new issues are filed — triage against gh_issues_agent_mission.md value streams and add to the appropriate sprint
when_not_to_use: Do not run gh_close.py without a committed fix — close only after the change is pushed
Do not use for PRs — this workflow is issues-only

related_agents.name: canvas_course_expert
related_agents.relevance: For issues about course audit or content quality

related_agents.name: canvas_content_sync
related_agents.relevance: For issues about push/pull failures or sync bugs
